cards.title: Parenting Makes Your Brain Younger and Healthier
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than non-parents. The mental and emotional demands of raising children appear to boost cognitive function.

cards.title: How Parenthood Sparked One Dad's Creativity
cards.body: A father discovered that having kids didn't kill his creativity—it actually enhanced it. Small moments, like his son's imaginative play, became sources of inspiration and wonder.

cards.title: Raising Boys Who Don't Like Sports
cards.body: Not all boys are athletic or enjoy traditional sports, yet athletic boys dominate childhood social landscapes. Parents need to support sons with different interests and strengths.

cards.title: Gender Transition: Mindfulness for Families
cards.body: When a child's gender identity differs from parental expectations, mindfulness and compassion become essential tools. These practices help families navigate transition with support and understanding.

cards.title: Teens With OCD: Playful Mindfulness Approach
cards.body: Mindfulness for teens with OCD can begin with laughter instead of silence. Simple games help young people practice attention, flexibility, and acceptance of mistakes.

cards.title: When Healthy Eating Becomes Unhealthy
cards.body: Pursuing perfect nutrition can quietly undermine health and create anxiety around food. Parents should teach children that food should nourish, not consume their thoughts.

cards.title: Meningitis B Jab for University Students
cards.body: The NHS offers a free meningitis B vaccination for students starting university. This follows the UK's largest meningitis B outbreak in Kent earlier this year.

cards.title: Adult Children Making Financial Demands
cards.body: When adult children guilt-trip parents with demands for money, clear boundaries are crucial. Understanding what drives this behavior helps parents respond with both compassion and firmness.
